---
title: "Untitled"
author: "LZR"
date: "2026-04-24"
output: html_document
runtime: shiny
---
```{r}
library(shiny)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)
library(purrr)
library(plotly)

raw <- read_csv("C:/Users/user/Desktop/world_air_quality.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

target_countries <- c("Ghana", "Mongolia", "India", "Iceland", "Japan", "Finland")

# Clean to daily means
# Remove obvious invalid values (e.g., sentinel codes such as -999, -995)
clean_daily <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    datetime = suppressWarnings(ymd_hm(`Last Updated`, tz = "UTC")),
    date = as.Date(datetime),
    country = str_trim(`Country Label`),
    pollutant = str_trim(Pollutant),
    value = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Value))
  ) %>%
  filter(country %in% target_countries) %>%
  filter(!is.na(date), !is.na(pollutant), !is.na(value)) %>%
  filter(value >= 0) %>%
  group_by(country, date, pollutant) %>%
  summarise(daily_mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

country_levels <- target_countries
pollutant_levels <- clean_daily %>%
  filter(pollutant != "PM2.5") %>%
  distinct(pollutant) %>%
  arrange(pollutant) %>%
  pull(pollutant)

# Build a complete country x pollutant grid so blank cells remain visible
cor_grid <- map_dfr(country_levels, function(cty) {
  wide <- clean_daily %>%
    filter(country == cty) %>%
    select(date, pollutant, daily_mean) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = pollutant, values_from = daily_mean)

  if (!"PM2.5" %in% names(wide)) {
    return(tibble(country = cty, pollutant = pollutant_levels, cor = NA_real_, n = NA_integer_))
  }

  map_dfr(pollutant_levels, function(p) {
    if (!p %in% names(wide)) {
      return(tibble(country = cty, pollutant = p, cor = NA_real_, n = NA_integer_))
    }

    x <- wide[["PM2.5"]]
    y <- wide[[p]]
    ok <- complete.cases(x, y)
    n <- sum(ok)
    cor_val <- if (n >= 3) cor(x[ok], y[ok], method = "pearson") else NA_real_
    tibble(country = cty, pollutant = p, cor = cor_val, n = n)
  })
}) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(country, levels = country_levels),
    pollutant = factor(pollutant, levels = pollutant_levels)
  )

decode_axis_value <- function(value, levels_vec) {
  if (length(value) == 0 || is.null(value) || all(is.na(value))) return(NA_character_)
  v <- as.character(value)[1]
  if (grepl("^[0-9]+$", v)) {
    idx <- as.integer(v)
    if (!is.na(idx) && idx >= 1 && idx <= length(levels_vec)) return(as.character(levels_vec[idx]))
  }
  v
}

make_pair_data <- function(dat, country_name, pollutant_name) {
  wide <- dat %>%
    filter(country == country_name) %>%
    select(date, pollutant, daily_mean) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = pollutant, values_from = daily_mean)

  if (!all(c("PM2.5", pollutant_name) %in% names(wide))) {
    return(tibble())
  }

  wide %>%
    select(date, PM2.5, all_of(pollutant_name)) %>%
    rename(other = all_of(pollutant_name)) %>%
    filter(complete.cases(PM2.5, other))
}

ui <- fluidPage(
  tags$head(tags$style(HTML("\n    .main-container { max-width: 1800px !important; }\n    .plotly, .shiny-plot-output { width: 100% !important; }\n  "))),
  titlePanel("PM2.5 Correlation Heatmap Across Countries"),
  fluidRow(
    column(
      width = 3,
      wellPanel(
        h4("Instructions"),
        p("This app computes daily means by country, date, and pollutant."),
        p("The heatmap shows Pearson correlations between PM2.5 and other pollutants."),
        p("Click any tile to generate the regression plot below."),
        checkboxInput("show_n", "Show paired-day counts", TRUE),
        tags$hr(),
        strong("Countries kept in the plot:"),
        p(paste(country_levels, collapse = ", ")),
        strong("Note:"),
        p("Ghana is kept in the axis even if some cells are blank because the source data may not have enough valid daily pairs.")
      )
    ),
    column(
      width = 9,
      plotlyOutput("cor_plot", height = "900px")
    )
  ),
  fluidRow(
    column(
      width = 12,
      verbatimTextOutput("selected_info"),
      plotOutput("reg_plot", height = "650px")
    )
  ),
  fluidRow(
    column(
      width = 12,
      tableOutput("cor_table")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  selected_cell <- reactiveVal(NULL)

  observeEvent(event_data("plotly_click", source = "hm"), {
    click <- event_data("plotly_click", source = "hm")
    req(click$x, click$y)

    selected_cell(list(
      country = decode_axis_value(click$x, country_levels),
      pollutant = decode_axis_value(click$y, pollutant_levels)
    ))
  })

  output$cor_plot <- renderPlotly({
    p <- ggplot(
      cor_grid,
      aes(
        x = country,
        y = pollutant,
        fill = cor,
        text = paste0(
          "Country: ", country,
          "<br>Pollutant: ", pollutant,
          "<br>Correlation: ", ifelse(is.na(cor), "NA", sprintf("%.2f", cor)),
          "<br>Paired days: ", ifelse(is.na(n), "NA", n)
        )
      )
    ) +
      geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.45) +
      geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(is.na(cor), "", sprintf("%.2f", cor))), size = 4) +
      scale_fill_gradient2(
        low = "#4575b4",
        mid = "white",
        high = "#d73027",
        midpoint = 0,
        limits = c(-1, 1),
        na.value = "grey90"
      ) +
      labs(
        title = "Correlation between PM2.5 and Other Pollutants by Country",
        x = "Country",
        y = "Pollutant",
        fill = "Correlation"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
      )

    ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text", source = "hm") %>%
      layout(margin = list(l = 120, r = 30, b = 120, t = 80))
  })

  output$cor_table <- renderTable({
    dat <- cor_grid %>% arrange(country, pollutant)
    if (isTRUE(input$show_n)) dat else dat %>% select(-n)
  }, digits = 3)

  output$selected_info <- renderText({
    sel <- selected_cell()
    if (is.null(sel)) {
      return("Click one tile in the heatmap to generate the regression plot.")
    }
    paste0("Selected: ", sel$country, " / ", sel$pollutant)
  })

  output$reg_plot <- renderPlot({
    sel <- selected_cell()
    req(!is.null(sel), !is.na(sel$country), !is.na(sel$pollutant))

    dat <- make_pair_data(clean_daily, sel$country, sel$pollutant)
    req(nrow(dat) >= 2)

    fit <- lm(other ~ PM2.5, data = dat)
    r2 <- summary(fit)$r.squared
    eq <- paste0("y = ", round(coef(fit)[1], 3), " + ", round(coef(fit)[2], 3), "x")

    ggplot(dat, aes(x = PM2.5, y = other)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
      labs(
        x = "Daily mean PM2.5",
        y = paste0("Daily mean ", sel$pollutant),
        title = paste0(sel$country, ": PM2.5 vs ", sel$pollutant, " Regression"),
        subtitle = paste0(eq, "   |   R² = ", round(r2, 3), "   |   n = ", nrow(dat))
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```


```{r}
library(shiny)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(plotly)

raw <- read_csv("C:/Users/user/Desktop/world_air_quality.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# ===== Data Processing =====
country_avg <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    country = str_trim(`Country Label`),
    pollutant = str_trim(Pollutant),
    value = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Value))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(value), value >= 0) %>%
  group_by(country, pollutant) %>%
  summarise(avg = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = pollutant, values_from = avg)

# Keep only pollutants with enough data
country_avg <- country_avg %>%
  select(where(~ sum(!is.na(.)) >= 5))

pollutants <- colnames(country_avg)[-1]

# Correlation matrix
cor_mat <- cor(country_avg[,-1], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

cor_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(cor_mat)) %>%
  rename(x = Var1, y = Var2, cor = Freq)

# Add unique key for click identification
cor_long$key <- paste(cor_long$x, cor_long$y, sep = "::")

# ===== UI =====
ui <- fluidPage(
  tags$head(tags$style(HTML("
    .container-fluid {max-width: 1800px;}
  "))),
  
  titlePanel("Cross-country Pollutant Correlation"),
  
  fluidRow(
    column(
      width = 12,
      plotlyOutput("heatmap", height = "1200px")
    )
  ),
  
  fluidRow(
    column(
      width = 12,
      verbatimTextOutput("info"),
      plotOutput("reg_plot", height = "650px")
    )
  )
)

# ===== SERVER =====
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  
  selected <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  
  observeEvent(event_data("plotly_click", source = "heatmap"), {
    click <- event_data("plotly_click", source = "heatmap")
    req(click$key)
    parts <- strsplit(click$key, "::", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    selected(list(
      x = parts[1],
      y = parts[2]
    ))
  })
  
  output$heatmap <- renderPlotly({
    
    p <- ggplot(cor_long, aes(x, y, fill = cor,
                             text = paste0(
                               "X: ", x,
                               "<br>Y: ", y,
                               "<br>Corr: ", round(cor, 2)
                             ),
                             key = key)) +
      geom_tile(color = "white") +
      
      geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", cor)),
                size = 3.5) +
      
      scale_fill_gradient2(
        low = "#4575b4",
        mid = "white",
        high = "#d73027",
        midpoint = 0,
        limits = c(-1,1)
      ) +
      
      coord_fixed() +
      
      labs(x = "Pollutant", y = "Pollutant") +   # Added axis labels
      
      theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
        panel.grid = element_blank()
      )
    
    ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text", source = "heatmap") %>%
      layout(margin = list(l = 100, r = 40, b = 120, t = 30))
  })
  
  output$info <- renderText({
    sel <- selected()
    if (is.null(sel)) return("Click a cell")
    paste0("Selected: ", sel$x, " vs ", sel$y)
  })
  
  output$reg_plot <- renderPlot({
    sel <- selected()
    req(sel)
    
    x_var <- sel$x
    y_var <- sel$y
    
    dat <- country_avg %>%
      select(country, all_of(x_var), all_of(y_var)) %>%
      rename(x = all_of(x_var), y = all_of(y_var)) %>%
      filter(complete.cases(x, y))
    
    req(nrow(dat) >= 3)
    
    fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = dat)
    
    ggplot(dat, aes(x, y)) +
      geom_point(size = 3) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
      geom_text(aes(label = country), vjust = -0.5, size = 4) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(x_var, " vs ", y_var),
        subtitle = paste0("y = ",
                          round(coef(fit)[1],2),
                          " + ",
                          round(coef(fit)[2],2),"x")
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```
